name: Treating a Cut Wound
description: How to properly stop bleeding, treat and bandage a cut wound in the field or at home.
label: FA

content.text: A cut wound is an injury caused by a sharp object. Proper treatment involves stopping the bleeding, disinfecting, and covering the wound. With heavy bleeding, the priority is applying a pressure bandage and calling emergency services.

1. Protect yourself — put on gloves before touching someone else's blood. 2. Stop the bleeding — apply a clean cloth and press directly on the wound for 5–10 minutes. Do not lift the cloth to check. 3. Clean the wound — rinse with clean water for 5–10 minutes, removing visible debris. 4. Disinfect — apply antiseptic around the wound, not inside it. 5. Cover — with a sterile dressing or bandage. For deeper wounds, seek medical attention.

Call emergency services if: bleeding cannot be stopped within 10 minutes, the wound is deeper than 1 cm or has ragged edges, the injured person is a child or elderly, the wound is on the face, hand, or joint, or it was caused by contact with rusty metal (tetanus risk).
